tasks.system: You are writing a README for a module package in a Next.js / Prisma monorepo. Output in markdown. No preamble.
tasks.prompt:
Write the README for **`packages/modules/broadcast/`** — the MBT Broadcast Module (Phase C Block 4).

Owns: podcast publishing (RSS + Buzzsprout distribution to Spotify/Apple/YouTube Music), TikTok Live orchestration (data model + Mac mini OBS bridge), clip extraction pipeline (LiveBroadcast → BroadcastClip → IG Reels + podcast highlights). Per-brand voice routing through the Voice System (Radio voice ≠ Magazine voice — never mix).

Must include: Purpose · Public API (`scheduleBroadcast`, `publishEpisode`, `syncToBuzzsprout`, `ingestClip`, `enrichClip`, `republishToSocial`) · Data model (PodcastShow, PodcastEpisode, LiveBroadcast, BroadcastClip — note Block 4 schema additions) · Buzzsprout integration (env: BUZZSPROUT_TOKEN, BUZZSPROUT_PODCAST_ID_RADIO) · Mac mini agent protocol (HMAC-secured polling at /api/broadcast/agent/poll) · Voice routing rules (Radio = music-first, distinct from Magazine = Inn guests) · Multi-tenant (every event scoped) · Usage example (TikTok Live → clip → IG Reels round trip) · Status (planned, depends on Block 1) · Testing.

Markdown only. ~1500 words.

tasks.system: You are writing a README for a module package. Output in markdown. No preamble.
tasks.prompt:
Write the README for **`packages/modules/social/`** — the MBT Social Media Module (Phase C Block 4 sister to Broadcast).

Owns: cross-platform social publishing (Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, X), per-brand voice routing + cross-brand contamination guards, Postiz bridge (Mac mini), platform-native fallbacks, scheduling, performance reporting.

Must include: Purpose · Public API (`schedulePost`, `publishImmediate`, `syncWithPostiz`, `getPerformance`, `republishClip`) · Voice routing — the dispatcher hard-fails if voice profile doesn't match destination integration (e.g., refuses to push Magazine voice to a Records IG account) · Postiz bridge (env: POSTIZ_API_URL, POSTIZ_API_KEY) · TikTok native API placeholder · Multi-tenant · Usage example · Status (planned, depends on Block 1 + 4) · Testing.

Markdown only. ~1300 words.

tasks.system: You are writing a README for a module package. Output in markdown. No preamble.
tasks.prompt:
Write the README for **`packages/modules/content-creation/`** — the MBT Content Creation Module (Phase C Block 6).

Owns: the AI content wizard backend — entity resolution from DirectoryBusiness, photo resolution from Immich CLIP search, prompt template loading from Sanity, AI agent orchestration via AI SDK v6 ToolLoopAgent, Sanity draft writing. Wraps the existing `apps/web/lib/ai-models.ts` failover chain — does not bypass.

Must include: Purpose · Public API (`wizardAgent`, `resolveEntities`, `searchPhotos`, `getTemplate`, `saveDraft`) · Content types supported (magazine-article, social-post, listing-description, episode-description, pitch-deck-section) · Per-content-type templates stored in Sanity (cached 5min) · Brand voice routing (Magazine ≠ Radio enforced at template level) · Entity resolution two-stage flow (search-term extraction via Gemini Flash → Prisma queries with visibility gate) · Immich CLIP integration (env: IMMICH_API_KEY) · Token budget (~$0.015 per magazine article) · Failure modes + retry · Status (planned, deepest module, 2-3 weeks) · Dependencies (Block 1 Commerce for billable usage tracking).

Markdown only. ~1700 words.

tasks.system: You are writing UI strings for an admin dashboard. Output in markdown sections that can be copy-pasted into React components. No preamble. Direct, professional, no marketing speak.
tasks.prompt:
Write all the UI strings for the **Subscriptions admin page** at `/admin/subscriptions` — list view, detail view, cancel modal, MRR dashboard.

Output the strings in the following structure:

```
## Page header
- title:
- subtitle:

## Tab labels
- All
- Active
- Past Due
- Trialing
- Canceled

## Filter labels

- Status filter:
- Customer email search:

- Plan
- Status

- Period end
- Actions

## Empty state

- description:
- CTA:

## Status badge labels

## Actions menu

## Cancel modal
```

tasks.system: You are writing customer transactional email templates. Output in markdown with clearly labeled emails. No preamble.
tasks.prompt:
Write the **subscription lifecycle transactional email templates** that the Commerce module sends to customers.

Five emails:

1. **Welcome / first payment confirmed** — sent right after Stripe webhook confirms first payment. Subject + body. Should reference: the brand they subscribed to, what they get, where to manage their subscription, who to email if issues.

2. **Payment failed / past due** — sent when invoice.payment_failed fires. Subject + body. Should reference: which subscription, retry attempt, when card will be retried, link to update payment method, soft empathetic tone (not threatening).

3. **Subscription cancelled (cancel-at-period-end)** — sent when customer cancels via portal. Subject + body. Should reference: when access ends, that they can resubscribe anytime, what they'll lose access to, soft 'we'd love to know why' link to feedback form.

4. **Trial ending in 3 days** — sent automatically 3 days before trial conversion. Subject + body. Should reference: when conversion happens, what the price will be, link to cancel before then.

5. **Annual renewal reminder** — sent 14 days before annual renewal. Subject + body. Should reference: renewal date, amount, link to manage / cancel.

For each email, write:
- Subject line
- Plain-text body (~150-250 words)
- Voice: warm, honest, brand-neutral (these get sent under different brands; copy uses [BRAND] placeholder)
- NO marketing-speak, NO 'Don't miss out!', NO emojis, NO 'limited time'

Markdown. ~1500 words total.

tasks.system: You are writing customer transactional email templates. Output in markdown with clearly labeled emails. No preamble.
tasks.prompt:
Write the **one-time Order lifecycle transactional email templates** for the Commerce module.

Four emails:

1. **Order received / payment confirmed** — sent after checkout.session.completed. Subject + body. Should reference: order number, items + quantities, total paid, what happens next, when to expect shipping/delivery, support contact.

2. **Order shipped** — sent when admin marks order shipped. Subject + body. Should reference: order number, tracking number + carrier, expected delivery window, what to do if not delivered.

3. **Order refunded** — sent after refund processed. Subject + body. Should reference: refund amount, original payment method (card ending in XXXX — placeholder), how long refund takes to appear, support contact.

4. **Cart abandoned** — sent 24h after a Stripe Checkout session expires unredeemed. Subject + body. Should reference: items they were looking at, link to complete purchase, soft 'no pressure' tone.

Same format as before: subject + body + ~150-250 words each. Brand-neutral with [BRAND] placeholder. Warm, honest. No marketing-speak. Markdown. ~1300 words total.

tasks.system: You are writing comprehensive technical documentation for a developer module. Output in markdown. No preamble.
tasks.prompt:
Write the **comprehensive operations runbook** for the MBT Coordination Layer Event Bus module (`packages/modules/events/`). This is the deeper companion to the README — covers operational concerns the README skips.

Must cover: 
1. Day-1 operator checklist — what to verify before declaring the module 'live' in prod (PgBoss schema present, worker route returns 200, smoke-test event delivers in <30s, dead-letter monitor running)
2. Common failures + recovery — handler timeouts, handler exceptions, PgBoss schema drift, Postgres connection saturation, multi-tenant isolation breach detection
3. Replay playbook — how to manually replay a dead-letter event, when to NOT replay (idempotency violations), how to bulk-replay after a downstream service recovers
4. Capacity planning — when PgBoss saturates (rule of thumb: ~1000 jobs/sec ceiling on shared Postgres before we'd want to migrate to Inngest or Vercel Queues), what monitoring to put in place, when to consider sharding by tenant
5. Adding a new event type — checklist (define in types.ts, register handlers in module init, add admin UI row, document in this README)
6. Adding a new handler — checklist (define handler function, register via events.on(), set tenantScope, set maxRetries based on operation cost, add observability log line, deploy)
7. Tenant-isolation audit query — the exact SQL query to run to detect cross-tenant leaks, when to schedule it (daily cron), what to alert on
8. Backup + recovery — Event table is durable audit log, PgBoss queue is ephemeral; recovery from a Postgres restore preserves the audit log, queue state is whatever was in flight

Voice: technical, calm, operations-first. Assume the reader is on-call at 2am and needs to fix something. Markdown. ~2500 words.

tasks.system: You are writing an operations runbook. Output in markdown. No preamble. Step-by-step, copy-pasteable commands.
tasks.prompt:
Write the **MBT Tenant Provisioning Runbook** — the step-by-step operator playbook for onboarding a new tenant onto the MBT platform. Vicki Wolpert (KW realtor in Athens NY) is the prototype customer; this runbook applies to her and every realtor / brokerage / civic partner that follows.

The target end-state of provisioning: customer has their own subdomain, their own dashboard at /admin, the right modules activated on their tenant, branding in place, Stripe billing relationship established, content templates loaded, photos in their Immich folder, social accounts connected via Postiz, first 30 days of social calendar populated.

Must include:

1. **Prerequisites** — what info we need from the customer before starting (business name, domain preference, Stripe payment method, social account credentials, brand assets, existing photos to import)
2. **Step-by-step provisioning** — numbered, with exact commands. Should reference these systems: Vercel domain setup, Cloudflare DNS, Sanity dataset (or shared dataset with tenant filter), Stripe customer creation, Postgres tenant row, Immich folder, Postiz account.
3. **Module activation** — for each MBT module (commerce, booking, broadcast, social, finance, events, content-creation), how to enable it for the tenant + the env vars/permissions/initial seed data each requires.
4. **First-30-days checklist** — what content gets pre-populated, what calls/check-ins happen with the customer, how performance metrics get baselined.
5. **Common gotchas** — DNS propagation timing, Stripe customer dedup if email already exists, Postiz OAuth window expiry, Immich folder permissions.
6. **Rollback** — if provisioning fails partway, how to clean up (delete the half-created Stripe customer, clean DNS, drop the tenant row, etc.).
7. **Verification** — what to test before declaring the tenant 'live' (load the subdomain, confirm dashboard renders, confirm a test order or subscription creates, confirm social schedule appears).
8. **Vicki Wolpert specific** — sample values for each field for the Vicki onboarding (subdomain: vickiwolpert.com, plan: Tuthill Pro tier, brand assets: KW Athens, etc.) so the operator has a copy-paste template.

Markdown. Include code blocks for commands. ~3000 words.

tasks.system: You are writing technical reference documentation. Output in markdown. No preamble.
tasks.prompt:
Write the **Stripe Webhook Events Reference** for the MBT Commerce module — the complete catalog of which Stripe webhook events the module handles, what each event triggers, and what env vars / config are required.

For each event type, include:
- Event type name (e.g., `checkout.session.completed`)
- When Stripe fires it
- What the Commerce module does in response (which Prisma table updated, which downstream side effects)
- Idempotency notes (what happens if the event fires twice)
- Failure mode if the handler errors

Cover at minimum these events:
- `checkout.session.completed` (subscription mode + payment mode)
- `customer.subscription.created`
- `customer.subscription.updated`
- `customer.subscription.deleted`
- `customer.subscription.trial_will_end`
- `invoice.created`
- `invoice.payment_succeeded`
- `invoice.payment_failed`
- `invoice.upcoming`
- `customer.created`
- `customer.updated`
- `customer.deleted`
- `payment_intent.succeeded`
- `payment_intent.payment_failed`
- `charge.refunded`
- `charge.dispute.created`

Also include: webhook signature verification flow, the COMMERCE_WEBHOOK_SECRET env var, retry policy on Stripe's side (Stripe retries with exponential backoff up to 3 days), and how to test webhooks locally (Stripe CLI `stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/api/commerce/webhook`).

Voice: technical, complete, reference-grade. Markdown. ~2500 words.
